evals.prompt: Run a full OWASP ASVS Level 1 security audit on our web application. It's a Next.js e-commerce site with user registration, product catalog, shopping cart, Stripe payment integration, and an admin dashboard. The app uses NextAuth for authentication, Prisma with PostgreSQL for the database, and is deployed on Vercel. We want to verify we meet baseline security before our public launch next month.
evals.expected_output: Determines ASVS Level 1 as the target (~70 requirements). Identifies all applicable chapters by scanning the codebase: V1 Encoding & Sanitization (always), V2 Validation & Business Logic (always), V3 Web Frontend (Next.js frontend exists), V4 API Security (API routes present), V5 File Handling (check for product image uploads), V6 Authentication (NextAuth present), V7 Session Management (NextAuth sessions), V8 Authorization (admin dashboard = role-based access), V9 Self-contained Tokens (check for JWT usage in NextAuth config), V10 OAuth & OIDC (check NextAuth providers), V11 Cryptography (password hashing, Stripe keys), V12 Secure Communication (HTTPS/TLS on Vercel), V13 Configuration (environment variables, Vercel settings), V14 Data Protection (PII from user registration, payment data), V15 Secure Coding (architecture review), V16 Security Logging (audit trails for orders/admin actions). Skips V17 WebRTC (no WebRTC). Runs each applicable chapter skill sequentially, collecting Pass/Fail/N/A for each L1 requirement. Generates a consolidated report at .claude/rubot/security-audit-report.md with overall compliance score, per-chapter matrix, all findings with ASVS requirement IDs and severity ratings (Critical/High/Medium/Low), and a phased remediation roadmap. Presents summary and offers next steps via AskUserQuestion.

evals.prompt: I need a targeted ASVS Level 2 security audit focusing only on authentication, authorization, and session management for our SaaS platform. We use Lucia auth with email/password and Google OAuth, PostgreSQL sessions stored in the database, and a custom RBAC system with admin/manager/member roles. We've had reports of users sometimes seeing other users' data after login.
evals.expected_output: Determines ASVS Level 2 as the target for the selected chapters. Narrows scope to three chapters: V6 Authentication (Lucia auth with email/password + Google OAuth), V7 Session Management (PostgreSQL-backed sessions), and V8 Authorization (custom RBAC with admin/manager/member roles). Also includes V10 OAuth & OIDC since Google OAuth is in use. Loads owasp-authentication skill and audits all L1+L2 requirements: credential storage (password hashing algorithm, salt), brute force protection (account lockout, rate limiting), MFA availability, credential recovery flow security, authentication error messages (generic vs specific). Loads owasp-session-management skill: session ID generation (entropy, length), session fixation on login, session timeout (idle and absolute), cookie flags (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite), concurrent session handling, session invalidation on logout and password change — pays special attention to session isolation given the reported data leakage. Loads owasp-authorization skill: RBAC implementation correctness, IDOR checks across admin/manager/member boundaries, privilege escalation tests, resource-level access control verification. Loads owasp-oauth-oidc skill: Google OAuth flow security, state parameter, PKCE usage, token validation, scope management. Generates focused audit report with compliance scores for the 4 audited chapters, findings prioritized around the reported session isolation issue, and remediation plan specifically targeting the cross-user data leakage.

evals.prompt: We're preparing to deploy a new multi-tenant SaaS application for healthcare appointment scheduling. It handles patient PII including names, emails, phone numbers, and appointment details. We need an ASVS security review to satisfy our compliance requirements before going to production. The stack is React frontend, Hono API on Cloudflare Workers, Neon PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM, Clerk for auth, and Stripe for billing. What level should we target and what does the audit cover?
evals.expected_output: Recommends ASVS Level 2 as the target given healthcare context with patient PII (names, emails, phones, appointment details), noting that Level 3 would be required if the application handled PHI under HIPAA (diagnosis codes, treatment records, insurance data) but Level 2 is appropriate for PII-level appointment scheduling data. Identifies all applicable chapters: V1 through V16 all apply, V17 WebRTC is skipped (no video/audio functionality). Explains the audit will cover ~175 L2 requirements across 16 chapters. Highlights chapters of particular importance for this use case: V6 Authentication (Clerk integration security, MFA enforcement for healthcare staff), V8 Authorization (multi-tenant isolation — critical for preventing cross-tenant patient data access), V11 Cryptography (encryption at rest for patient PII in Neon PostgreSQL), V12 Secure Communication (TLS for all Cloudflare Workers endpoints), V13 Configuration (Cloudflare Workers security settings, environment secrets), V14 Data Protection (PII handling, data minimization, retention policies for patient data), V16 Security Logging (audit trail for patient record access — important for compliance). Runs all 16 applicable chapter audits, generates full compliance report with per-chapter scores, maps findings to compliance requirements, and produces a remediation roadmap with healthcare-specific prioritization (patient data protection findings elevated in severity).
